parentQuestion: Minerals required by the body in fairly large amounts are called ___.

scaffolds.q: What are minerals in the context of nutrition?
scaffolds.explain: Minerals are INORGANIC elements — simple atoms from the periodic table. they come from rocks, soil, and water, absorbed by plants and passed through the food chain. Unlike vitamins (which are ORGANIC compounds with carbon-hydrogen bonds), minerals are elemental: Ca, Fe, Na, K, Mg, Zn. Breathe in this knowledge:this: you're built from stardust and geology.

scaffolds.q: Which mineral is most abundant in the human body and essential for bone structure?
scaffolds.explain: Calcium is the MOST abundant mineral in the body — about 1-2% of body weight (roughly 1-2 kg in an adult!). about 99% is stored in bones and teeth as hydroxyapatite (Ca₁₀(PO₄)₆(OH)₂), providing rigid structural support. The remaining 1%? Critical for nerve signals, muscle contractions, blood clotting, enzyme regulation. Accept this reality:this: bulk mineral, MASSIVE importance.
